revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 6 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 6-ngie̍t]][[ja:6月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 6월]][[th:เดือน 6]][[za:Ndwenloeg]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 6 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:六月]][[zh:农历六月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 6 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 6-ngie̍t]][[ja:6月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 6월]][[th:เดือน 6]][[za:Ndwenloeg]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 6 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:六月]][[zh:农历六月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 6 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 6-ngie̍t]][[ja:6月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 6월]][[th:เดือน 6]][[za:Ndwenloeg]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 6 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:六月]][[zh:农历六月]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 6 nguŏk]][[hak:Nùng-la̍k 6-ngie̍t]][[ja:6月 (旧暦)]][[ko:음력 6월]][[th:เดือน 6]][[za:Ndwenloeg]][[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 6 goe̍h]][[zh-yue:六月]][[zh:农历六月]]
{{Lunisolar June|qq=}}'''Lunisolar June''' (旧暦6月) is the sixth {{ill|Month|en|Month|qid=Q5151|lt=Month (calendar)|1=Month}} of the {{ill|Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} ({{ill|Lunisolar calendar|en|Lunisolar calendar|qid=Q194235|lt=Lunisolar calendar|1=Lunisolar calendar}}). Before the adoption of the {{ill|Tenpō calendar|en|Tenpō calendar|qid=Q1066717|lt=Tenpō calendar|1=Tenpō calendar}}, Lunisolar June was defined as the month containing {{ill|Dashu (solar term)|en|Dashu (solar term)|qid=Q1147765|lt=Dashu|1=Dashu (solar term)}}. In the {{ill|Gregorian calendar|en|Gregorian calendar|qid=Q12138|lt=New Calendar|1=Gregorian calendar}}, it corresponds roughly to late {{ill|June|en|June|qid=Q120|lt=June|1=June}} through early {{ill|August|en|August|qid=Q122|lt=August|1=August}}.
Lunisolar June is also called {{ill|Minazuki|en|Minazuki|qid=Q10905767|lt=Minazuki|1=Minazuki}}. For the etymology, see [[#Etymology of traditional Japanese name]]{{efn|June|ja|6月|qq=draft}}. Another name for this month is {{ill|Kika|ja|季夏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kika|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 6月 (旧暦)|1=June (Lunisolar)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q839146}}.
In the East Asian lunisolar calendar, the number of days in each month ({{ill|Days in a lunar month|ja|月の大小|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Days in a lunar month|1=short month|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11515765}}, 30-day "big months" and 29-day "small months") varies by year. As a result, some years have only 29 days in Lunisolar June, making {{ill|June 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|6月30日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}} nonexistent.
==Etymology of traditional Japanese name==
In {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}, the {{ill|June (Lunisolar)|ja|6月 (旧暦)|lt=Lunisolar June|lt_ja=旧暦6月|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June (Lunisolar)|qq=}} is also called '''Minazuki''' (みなづき). However, originally it was an alternate name for the lunar sixth month.
In the Asahikawa dialect of the {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Hokkaido Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}, June is called “Toytachup” (トイタチュㇷ゚; {{ill|Ainu language|en|Ainu language|qid=Q27969|lt=Ainu language|1=Ainu language}}: toyta cup), meaning “month of tilling the fields”<ref>[https://ainugo.nam.go.jp/siror/monthly/201510.html#01 《ことばからみたアイヌ文化と自然２》アイヌ文化における時間使用 高橋 靖以（北海道大学アイヌ・先住民研究センター博士研究員＝特別寄稿） 月刊シロㇿ]</ref>.
The English month name {{lang|en|June}} comes from {{ill|Juno (mythology)|en|Juno (mythology)|qid=Q125046|lt=Juno|1=Juno (mythology)}} (Juno), the wife of {{ill|Jupiter (god)|en|Jupiter (god)|qid=Q4649|lt=Jupiter|1=Jupiter (god)}} (Jupiter), in {{ill|Roman mythology|en|Roman mythology|qid=Q122173|lt=Roman mythology|1=Roman mythology}}. Since Juno is the protector of {{ill|married life|ja|結婚生活|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/married life|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, brides who marry in June are called “June bride” ({{lang|en|June bride}}), and it is said that those who marry in this month will be happy. However, there is a theory that in {{ill|Europe|en|Europe|qid=Q46|lt=European|1=Europe}} countries with many {{ill|Catholicism|en|Catholicism|qid=Q1841|lt=Catholicism|1=Catholicism}} believers, it is unlikely that {{ill|Greek mythology|ja|ギリシャ神話|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Greek mythology|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ギリシア神話|1=Greek mythology|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q34726}} or Roman pagan gods would influence people’s lives<ref>[https://toyokeizai.net/articles/-/174675 フランス人の結婚は｢ロマンのかけら｣もない・参列者の前で｢財産分与法｣すら読み上げる！] 岩本麻奈-東洋経済（2017年6月4日閲覧）</ref>.
In Japan, the concept of the “June bride” was initiated by the {{ill|bridal|ja|ブライダル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/bridal|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 結婚|1=Marriage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8445}} industry around the 1970s, which was concerned that few people married in June due to the many rainy, humid, and gloomy days; before that, it was not known（Unlike Japan, in Europe, June is the season when the long winter has ended and flowers begin to bloom, creating an open and bright mood across society）.
== Dates of Minazuki == {| class="wikitable" |+ Dates of Minazuki ! Year !! New Moon !! Last Day !! Days |- | 2014 || {{ill|June 27|en|June 27|qid=Q2664|lt=June 27|1=June 27}} || {{ill|July 26|en|July 26|qid=Q2723|lt=July 26|1=July 26}} || 30 |- | 2015 || {{ill|July 16|en|July 16|qid=Q2731|lt=July 16|1=July 16}} || {{ill|August 13|en|August 13|qid=Q2776|lt=August 13|1=August 13}} || 29 |- | 2016 || {{ill|July 4|en|July 4|qid=Q2698|lt=July 4|1=July 4}} || {{ill|August 2|en|August 2|qid=Q2785|lt=August 2|1=August 2}} || 30 |- | 2017 || {{ill|July 23|en|July 23|qid=Q2722|lt=July 23|1=July 23}} || {{ill|August 21|en|August 21|qid=Q2823|lt=August 21|1=August 21}} || 30 |- | 2018 || {{ill|July 13|en|July 13|qid=Q2687|lt=July 13|1=July 13}} || {{ill|August 10|en|August 10|qid=Q2779|lt=August 10|1=August 10}} || 29 |- | 2019 || {{ill|July 3|en|July 3|qid=Q2699|lt=July 3|1=July 3}} || {{ill|July 31|en|July 31|qid=Q2715|lt=July 31|1=July 31}} || 29 |- | 2020 || {{ill|July 21|en|July 21|qid=Q2727|lt=July 21|1=July 21}} || {{ill|August 18|en|August 18|qid=Q2826|lt=August 18|1=August 18}} || 29 |- | 2021 || {{ill|July 10|en|July 10|qid=Q2689|lt=July 10|1=July 10}} || {{ill|August 7|en|August 7|qid=Q2781|lt=August 7|1=August 7}} || 29 |- | 2022 || {{ill|June 29|en|June 29|qid=Q2659|lt=June 29|1=June 29}} || {{ill|July 28|en|July 28|qid=Q2718|lt=July 28|1=July 28}} || 30 |- | 2023 || {{ill|July 18|en|July 18|qid=Q2730|lt=July 18|1=July 18}} || {{ill|August 15|en|August 15|qid=Q2774|lt=August 15|1=August 15}} || 29 |- | 2024 || {{ill|July 6|en|July 6|qid=Q2695|lt=July 6|1=July 6}} || {{ill|August 3|en|August 3|qid=Q2786|lt=August 3|1=August 3}} || 29 |- | 2025 || {{ill|June 25|en|June 25|qid=Q2662|lt=June 25|1=June 25}} || {{ill|July 24|en|July 24|qid=Q2721|lt=July 24|1=July 24}} || 30 |- | 2025 ([[:en:Leap month|leap]]) || {{ill|July 25|en|July 25|qid=Q2720|lt=July 25|1=July 25}} || {{ill|August 22|en|August 22|qid=Q2828|lt=August 22|1=August 22}} || 29 |}
== See Also ==
* {{ill|Month (lunar calendar)|ja|月 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Month (lunar calendar)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135505768|1=month of the Chinese calendar}} * {{ill|List of days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|ja|旧暦の日付の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of days of the Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}} * {{ill|June 30 (Lunisolar)|ja|6月30日 (旧暦)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/June 30 (Lunisolar)|qq=}}
== References == === Citations === {{reflist|qq=}}
=== Sources === {{refbegin|qq=}} *{{cite journal |last=Ariga |first=Natsuki |authorlink=:ja:有賀夏紀 |title=Kongō-ji zō 'Ryūō-kōshiki' no shikibun sekai: shakuronchūshaku to kiugirei wo megutte |script-title=ja:金剛寺蔵『龍王講式』の式文世界 : 釈論Noteと祈雨儀礼をめぐって |trans-title=The study of Ryūō-kōshiki at Kongō-ji Temple : Consideration into the influence of Syakumakaenron and its commentaries and the rituals to pray |journal=Jinbun / Gakushuin University Research Institute for Humanities-journal |volume=18 |date=March 2020 |url=https://glim-re.repo.nii.ac.jp/?action=repository_uri&item_id=4821&file_id=22 |pages=166–180 |hdl=10959/00004813 |publisher= |qq=}} * {{cite journal |last=Faure |first=Bernard R. |author-link=Bernard Faure |title=Pan Gu and his descendants: Chinese cosmology in medieval Japan |script-title=zh:盤古及其後代：論日本中古時代的中國宇宙論 |journal=Taiwan Journal of East Asian Studies |volume=2 |number=1 |date=June 2005 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=xdsQAQAAMAAJ&q=%22yellow+dragon%22 |pages=71–88 |doi=10.7916/D8V40THT |qq=}} [http://tjeas.ciss.ntnu.edu.tw/zh-tw/shared/redirect/25?folder=journals&file=2-1-3.pdf pdf] @ National Taiwan Normal University * {{cite book |last=Fowler |first=Jeanine D. |author-link= |year=2005 |title=An Introduction to the Philosophy and Religion of Taoism: Pathways to Immortality |publisher=Sussex Academic Press |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=9wi-ZDdmaqEC |url-status= |isbn=1845190866 |qq=}}{{deadlink|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Iwata |first=Masaru |author-link=:ja:岩田勝 |title=Kagura genryū kō |script-title=ja:神楽源流考 |publisher=Meicho shuppan |date=1983 |url=https://books.goog岩田le.com/books?id=xvZNAQAAIAAJ&q=龍王 |pages= |isbn= |qq=}} * {{citation |last=Monta |first=Seiichi |author-link=:ja:門田誠一 |title=Nihon kodai ni okeru gohōryū kankei shutsudo moji shiryō no shiteki haikei |script-title=ja:日本古代における五方龍関係出土文字史料の史的背景 |journal=Bukkyō Daigaku Shūkyō Bunka Myūjiamu Kenkyūj Kiyō |volume=8 |date=2012-03-30 |publisher= |url=http://archives.bukkyo-u.ac.jp/rp-contents/SB/0008/SB00080R005.pdf |qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Nikaido |first=Yoshihiro |year=2015 |title=Asian Folk Religion and Cultural Interaction |publisher=Vandenhoeck & Ruprecht |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=TozUCgAAQBAJ |isbn=978-3847004851 |qq=}} * {{cite book |last = Overmyer |first = Daniel L. |year = 2009 |title = Local Religion in North China in the Twentieth Century the Structure and Organization of Community Rituals and Beliefs |publisher = Brill |location = Leiden, South Holland; Boston, Massachusetts |url = http://cnqzu.com/library/To%20Organize/Books/Brill%20Ebooks/Brill._Handbook_of_Oriental_Studies/Brill.%20Handbook%20of%20Oriental%20Studies/Local_Religion_in_North_China_in_the_Twentieth_Century__Handbook_of_Oriental_Studies_.pdf |isbn = 9789047429364 |access-date = 2018-03-03 |archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20150616021930/http://cnqzu.com/library/To%20Organize/Books/Brill%20Ebooks/Brill._Handbook_of_Oriental_Studies/Brill.%20Handbook%20of%20Oriental%20Studies/Local_Religion_in_North_China_in_the_Twentieth_Century__Handbook_of_Oriental_Studies_.pdf |archive-date = 2015-06-16 |url-status = dead |qq=}} * {{cite journal|last=Ruppert |first=Brian O. |author-link= |title=Buddhist Rainmaking in Early Japan: The Dragon King and the Ritual Careers of Esoteric Monks |journal=History of Religions |volume=42 |number=2 |date=November 2002 |url= |pages=143–174 |doi=10.1086/463701 |jstor=3176409|s2cid=161794053 |qq=}} * {{cite book|last=Sakade |first=Yoshinobu |authorlink=:ja:坂出祥伸 |title=Nihon to dōkyō bunka |script-title=ja:日本と道教文化 |publisher=Kadokawa shoten |date=2010 |url= |series=|qq=}} * {{cite book |last=Tom |first=K. S. |year=1989 |title=Echoes from Old China: Life, Legends, and Lore of the Middle Kingdom |publisher=University of Hawaii Press |isbn=0824812859 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=j84ysksfk28C&pg=PA55 |access-date=2023-09-25|qq=}} * {{cite journal |last=Trenson |first=Steven |author-link= |title=Rice, Relics, and Jewels |journal=Japanese Journal of Religious Studies |volume=35 |number=2 |date=2018 |url= https://core.ac.uk/download/pdf/228737341.pdf |pages=269–308 |jstor=26854486 |qq=}} * {{cite journal |last=Trenson |first=Steven |author-link= |title=Une analyse critique de l'histoire du Shōugyōhō et du Kujakukyōhō : rites ésotériques de la pluie dans le Japon de l'époque de Heian |journal=Cahiers d'Extrême-Asie |volume=13 |date=2002 |url=https://www.persee.fr/docAsPDF/asie_0766-1177_2002_num_13_1_1191.pdf |pages=455–495 |doi=10.3406/asie.2002.1191 |lang=fr |qq=}} *{{Cite thesis |ref={{SfnRef|Zhang|2014|qq=}} |author=Zhang Lishan |author-link= |title=Higashi ajia ni okeru Dokō shinkō to bunka kōshō |script-title=ja:東アジアにおける土公信仰と文化交渉 |date=2014-03-31 |publisher=Kansai University |url=https://kansai-u.repo.nii.ac.jp/?action=repository_action_common_download&item_id=249&item_no=1&attribute_id=19&file_no=1 |doi=10.32286/00000236 |qq=}} {{refend|qq=}}

If I get anywhere near implementing it, I will do so based on the actual completion tiers of this wiki rather than whether I added them when they were on simple english wikipedia or wherever--><!--人文--><!--学習院大学人文科学研究所--><!--臺灣東亞文明硏究學刊--><!--Jeanine D. Fowler--><!--名著出版--><!--佛教大学宗教文化ミュージアム研究紀要--><!--佛教大学--><!--Brian O. Ruppert--><!--角川書店--><!--no preview (Amazon's preview does not include relevant pages)--><!--角川選書--><!--Steven Trenson--><!--Steven Trenson--><!--張麗山--><!--関西大学--> <references group="lower-alpha"/> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 6 nguŏk]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 6-ngie̍t]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 6 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:六月]] <!--zhwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[cdo:Nùng-lĭk 6 nguŏk]] [[hak:Nùng-la̍k 6-ngie̍t]] [[zh-min-nan:Kū-le̍k 6 goe̍h]] [[zh-yue:六月]] {{wikidata link|Q839146}} [[Category:Buddhist texts]] [[Category:Chinese calendars]] [[Category:Lunisolar June]] [[Category:Lunisolar months]] [[Category:Taoism]] [[Category:Taoism in Japan]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Japanese Calendar]]
